Our services

Russell-Cooke wins Law Firm of the Year at the LexisNexis Legal Awards 2026

We were proud to be named Law Firm of the Year at the LexisNexis Legal Awards 2026. The award recognises our continued growth and commitment to our clients, people and communities.

Find out more

Responsible Business Report 25-26

At Russell-Cooke, we are committed to doing the right thing for our people, communities and the environment, and support those seeking access to justice. In this year's report, we chart our progress against last year's objectives and outline our targets for the year ahead.

To find out more, read our 2025-6 Responsible Business report.

Find out more

In the spotlight

Birth Trauma Awareness Week 2026

As a Trusted Legal Partner of the Birth Trauma Association, we are proud to support Birth Trauma Awareness Week 2026. Birth trauma can have lasting physical and psychological consequences for women and their families, especially where negligent care leads to avoidable harm. However, some of the most debilitating costs of birth trauma are hidden. In this special feature, members of our medical negligence team explore the hidden cost of birth trauma and the complex issues this can bring up for families navigating life after a difficult birth.

Find out more

Commercial contracts and terms of business

Corporate and commercial law

Commercial contracts and terms of business

Enquiry form

Corporate and commercial team

An introduction and overview to commercial contracts and terms of business

Our specialist team provides commercially focused legal advice tailored to your business requirements. We create and negotiate the contracts your business requires to successfully manage relationships with customers, suppliers and collaborators.

How our solicitors can help you with commercial contracts and terms of business

Key services offered

We advise on a range of commercial and business agreements including supplier, manufacturing and distribution agreements, complex framework agreements, brand licensing and franchising agreements, sponsorship agreements, terms and conditions and website policies, online terms and contracts and non-disclosure agreements.

We can also advise on issues arising from public procurement rules.

Why choose Russell-Cooke for commercial contracts and terms of business

About our corporate and commercial team

We understand that every business is different and our team offers a tailored and constructive service to meet your requirements.

We provide clear and concise, practical and responsive advice and have the expertise to provide the protection your business requires.

Industry expertise

We have particular experience advising clients in the following business sectors:

technology, media and telecommunications (TMT)

retail and leisure

travel

recruitment

education

consumer goods

automated vending

Examples of our work include:

Supplier contracts: advising a multi-outlet retail client on a full suite of supplier contracts. The work involved preparing separate documents for different contexts including the supply of goods, services and software. We also frequently prepare template documents to allow for supplier procurement and engagement with less hassle and less risk.

Customer contracts: we assisted a national vending machine supplier with a multi-million pound per-year contract for the supply of vending machines, including a complex pricing and rebate mechanism.

Moving contracts online: we assisted a telecoms supplier in taking existing paper-based contracts and revising and amending those documents to work online. We also advised on the development of the online sign-up process.

Startups: we help startups  with commercial and consumer agreements they need to manage risk from day one. Recent startups we have assisted include: software suppliers to the financial services sector, package holiday suppliers, e-commerce operators and business services providers.

Corporate and commercial team

Charging information

Charges are usually based on our hourly rates plus administrative charges and disbursements. We will provide you with an initial estimate of your likely legal costs and will update this as your requirements or the circumstances of the work change.

We may also be able to offer fixed priced options for clients depending on their budget and the size, type and complexity of the work involved.

We would be pleased to discuss your requirements free of charge and tailor a costs proposal accordingly.

Services

Legal advice for startups

Our expert startups team has extensive experience helping new businesses establish a strong legal foundation, providing specialist commercial legal advice tailored to startups and early-stage ventures.

Learn more

Get in touch

If you would like to speak with a member of the team you can contact our corporate and commercial solicitors by telephone on +44 (0)20 3826 7511 or complete our enquiry form.

Building families—legal advice

Family and children

Building families—legal advice

Enquiry form

Family and children team

Introduction and overview to building a family

We provide support and tailored advice for every aspect of building a family. We understand how important family is and approach every case with sensitivity and care. Children’s welfare is at the heart of everything we do.

We have experience in domestic and international family arrangements and our lawyers are specialists in children law. This means we have the expertise to help you overcome any challenge and enable you to create your family in your own way.

How our solicitors can help you with building a family

Key services offered

Our specialists can offer advice and representation if you are creating your family in one of the following ways: 

surrogacy (here or abroad): with the assistance of our in-house immigration and private client lawyers we are able to help with all stages of the surrogacy process

obtaining an adoption order or if you are seeking advice about the law in relation to adoption

obtaining a parental order 

We can also help if you are seeking advice about:

undergoing assisted reproduction at a clinic or outside of a clinic 

a donor conception agreement

a dispute regarding the legal status of biological and non-biological parents 

the law in relation to fertility treatment

disputes about arrangements to share the care of children conceived through fertility treatment or conception agreements

Why choose Russell-Cooke for legal advice on building families?

About our family law team

We are one of few child law teams which exist separately from wider family law practice, whilst being able to work with our family colleagues if and when required. This means that we are on top of all aspects of children law and practice – it is what we do day in, day out.

We focus on the best interests of the child at all times and our aim is to be constructive and provide solutions using our knowledge and experience. We work closely with professional experts and judges. 

We are market leaders – Chambers and Partners legal directory ranked us highly for public children law, private children law and cross-border disputes concerning children.

In a landmark surrogacy case we secured the first parental order establishing legal parenthood of an adult child born through a surrogacy arrangement influencing a change in law. 

We are trained advocates who regularly represent clients in court proceedings. This means that we are there for our clients inside and outside of the courtroom.

Family and children team

Testimonials and legal directory quotes

Here are some comments made about us.

Charging information

Our hourly rates are competitive, offering value for money.

Charges are usually based on our hourly rates plus administrative charges and disbursements.

We offer a range of experience levels and costs so that everyone we work for has options, whilst the most experienced members of the team offer support throughout.  We will provide you with an initial estimate of your likely legal costs and will update this as your requirements or the circumstances of the work change. The current range of hourly rates for members of the team is from £200 plus VAT to £400 plus VAT per hour. We may be able to offer a fixed fee meeting. Please enquire for more details. 

Get in touch

If you would like to speak with a member of the team you can contact our family and children solicitors by telephone on +44 (0)20 3826 7520 or complete our enquiry form.
